conservationHero.label: Conservation
conservationHero.heading: Protecting the Landscapes That Inspire Exploration.
conservationHero.subheading: Afari Trails supports a future where tourism, storytelling, and conservation exist in balance with Africa's wilderness and communities.
conservationHero.imageAlt: Elephants in golden African wilderness at sunrise

conservationPhilosophy.label: Our Philosophy
conservationPhilosophy.statement: We believe meaningful exploration must contribute to the protection of landscapes, wildlife, and cultural heritage for future generations.
conservationPhilosophy.imageAlt: African wilderness landscape at golden hour
themes: Coexistence between people and wild places
Preservation rooted in respect, not performance
Ethical tourism shaped by local knowledge
Long-term collaboration with communities and ecosystems

items.title: Wildlife Protection
items.description: Supporting initiatives that safeguard species and the habitats they depend on.

items.title: Habitat Preservation
items.description: Protecting wetlands, savannas, and river corridors essential to African biodiversity.

items.title: Community Partnerships
items.description: Working alongside local people as collaborators — not subjects of a narrative.

items.title: Sustainable Tourism
items.description: Low-impact exploration designed to leave places stronger than we found them.

items.title: Education & Storytelling
items.description: Using narrative, media, and field experience to deepen conservation awareness.

items.title: Cultural Preservation
items.description: Honoring heritage, craft, and identity as inseparable from environmental stewardship.

conservationCommunity.label: Community & Local Impact
conservationCommunity.heading: Conservation is human, too.
conservationCommunity.body: Wild places endure when the people who live beside them thrive. Afari Trails supports local guides, artisans, and community-led initiatives — building ethical partnerships, fair employment, and cultural preservation that travel alongside every expedition and venture. We do not speak for communities. We listen, collaborate, and invest in shared outcomes.
conservationCommunity.imageAlt: Community members in warm natural light

conservationPartners.label: Partners & Collaborations
conservationPartners.heading: Building alliances with purpose.
conservationPartners.body: Afari Trails is actively seeking partnerships that support responsible exploration and long-term conservation impact across Africa.

principles.title: Low-Impact Exploration
principles.description: Travel designed to tread lightly and think deeply.

principles.title: Ethical Partnerships
principles.description: Relationships built on trust, transparency, and mutual respect.

principles.title: Conservation Awareness
principles.description: Every journey carries a responsibility to understand and protect.

principles.title: Community Collaboration
principles.description: Local voices at the center — never at the margins.

conservationInvolvement.heading: Become Part of the Journey
conservationInvolvement.body: Whether you lead conservation work, shape hospitality, or share our belief that exploration must protect what it depends on — we welcome the conversation.

conservationClosing.quote: The future of exploration depends on the future of the wilderness.
